summary: Improved matching for the lbmemory translation unit by replacing opaque allocator padding with structured fields, adding exact string/data references, decompiling the relocation callback path and ARAM allocator init routine, and clarifying verified Handle semantics. The PR was closed without a recorded merge, but the bot report showed 4 new matches and one major unmatched-function improvement.

key_files.role: Main decompilation work: allocator struct refinement, string/data symbol placement, relocation/alarm callback decompilation, ARAM allocator initialization, and assertion/codegen adjustments.
Handle field comment update clarifying that x8_hi is an arena high bound for heap handles and allocation size for child handles.

what_changed: Added typed internal structures for lbmemory: MemEntry, LBMgr, and a more detailed Allocator layout.
Replaced several allocator padding byte arrays with typed fields: x8_mem[0x83], x638_heap[6], and x6A0_mgr.
Changed the allocator global from a static anonymous g_alloc to symbol-backed lbMemory_804318B0 with #define g_alloc lbMemory_804318B0.
Added STATIC_ASSERT checks for sizeof(struct MemEntry) == 0xC and sizeof(lbMemory_804318B0) == 0x6F0.

Added explicit .data and .sdata string symbols used by assertions and OSReport, including lbl_803BA2C0, lbl_803BA2CC, lbl_803BA2DC, lbl_803BA328, lbl_803BA338, lbl_803BA344, lbl_803BA368, lbMemory_804D3788, lbMemory_804D3790, and lbMemory_804D3794.
Updated assertions to use the explicit string symbols instead of __FILE__ or literal strings where that improved data matching.
Moved LBMgr access from pointer arithmetic on &g_alloc + 0x6A0 to the typed field &g_alloc.x6A0_mgr.
Improved fn_80015184 to use typed LBMgr fields and changed OSSetAlarm from a literal tick value/NULL callback to OSMillisecondsToTicks(3) and fn_80015184.
Added a decompiled body for lbMemory_80015320, handling relocation of handles via HSD_DevComRequest for addresses below 0x80000000 and staged alarm-driven copy setup for other addresses.
Added lbMemory_8001564C, initializing ARAM bounds, free MemEntry list, free heap handles, x69C, and related allocator counters/fields.
Used #pragma dont_inline around lbMemory_8001564C, indicating inlining control was relevant to matching.
Removed speculative comments in lbmemory.c around list behavior, malloc behavior, alignment, and Handle interpretation.
Updated lbmemory.h so Handle::x8_hi is documented as dual-use: arena high bound for heap handles and allocation size for child handles.
smart_moves: Replacing raw padding with typed arrays made later code expressible through field access while preserving the exact allocator size with static assertions.
Symbolizing assertion and report strings helped recover .data/.sdata matches instead of relying on compiler-generated or merged literals.
Using a real LBMgr field avoided brittle pointer arithmetic at offset 0x6A0 and made fn_80015184/lbMemory_80015320 easier to reason about.
The Handle::x8_hi comment was narrowed to behavior evidenced by code instead of leaving a speculative 'hi or size' question.
The PR separated fully matched gains from partial work: bot output showed lbMemory_8001564C, fn_80015184, .data, and .sdata at 100%, while lbMemory_80015320 remained partial.
Using OSMillisecondsToTicks(3) instead of a raw constant improved semantic clarity while still matching the alarm timing expression.

The lbMemory_80015320 implementation captured asynchronous relocation structure: immediate DevCom request for one address class, alarm-managed chunk copy for the other, then final callback through alloc->x6E8.
Leaving lbMemory_80014FC8 and lbMemory_80015320 listed as remaining non-perfect matches prevented overclaiming and documented the exact remaining matching blockers.
decomp_lessons: When a global allocator is mostly known, gradually replace padding with typed fields and guard layout with STATIC_ASSERTs to avoid silent offset drift.
For data-section matching, explicit named string objects can be necessary even when ordinary string literals are semantically equivalent.
Dual-purpose struct fields should be documented by observed behavior, not guessed names; this PR records x8_hi as high-bound for heap handles and size for child handles.
Typed access to embedded structs often improves both readability and codegen compared with repeated base-plus-offset casts.
Use project macros/functions such as OSMillisecondsToTicks when they express the original constant and produce matching code.
Do not keep speculative comments once a field or list behavior has been verified; noisy guesses can mislead later decomp work.
For allocator initialization functions, matching may require explicit manual initialization of freelist links and base-offset stores rather than abstract helper loops for every field.
Partial matches should be documented with exact percentages and the suspected cause, such as saved-register allocation or instruction scheduling, so future attempts can resume efficiently.
naming_conventions: Internal unknown/partially known struct fields kept offset-style names such as x0_next, x4_lo, x8_hi, x62C_free_mem, x638_heap, x6A0_mgr, and x6E8.
Recovered global symbols followed the lbMemory address naming pattern, e.g. lbMemory_804318B0, lbMemory_804D3788, lbMemory_804D3790, and lbMemory_804D3794.
Anonymous data labels retained linker-style names such as lbl_803BA2C0 and lbl_803BA344 when semantic names were not established.
Function names stayed address-based for still-unnamed functions, e.g. lbMemory_80015320 and lbMemory_8001564C.
The local macro PUSH_HANDLE remained uppercase macro style for freelist push behavior.
Struct type names used concise semantic names where evidence was strong: MemEntry, LBMgr, and Allocator.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added explicit char arrays with exact sizes for string data to force expected .data/.sdata layout.
Used STATIC_ASSERTs to verify recovered struct sizes and prevent layout regressions.
Introduced #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around lbMemory_8001564C to control code generation.

Preserved some raw base-offset writes in lbMemory_8001564C, such as writes at base + 0x638 through base + 0x688 and base + 0x6D0, likely because direct struct/member abstraction did not yet produce the desired codegen.
Used 32-byte alignment expression ((size + 0x1F) & 0xFFFFFFE0) for allocation and relocation sizes.
Matched assertion callsites by passing explicit recovered strings rather than __FILE__ and string literals.
Changed fn_80015184 alarm scheduling to OSSetAlarm(&mgr->alarm, OSMillisecondsToTicks(3), fn_80015184), matching both semantics and likely expected call shape.
Bot report measured new matches: lbMemory_8001564C at 100%, fn_80015184 at 100%, .data at 100%, .sdata at 100%, and lbMemory_80015320 improved to 96.11%.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code increased by 924 bytes and matched data by 216 bytes for GALE01.
Reported 4 new matches: lbMemory_8001564C, fn_80015184, .data, and .sdata in main/melee/lb/lbmemory.
Reported lbMemory_80015320 improved from 0.00% to 96.11% but remained unmatched.

Remaining issues were explicitly identified as lbMemory_80014FC8 at 99.324326% and lbMemory_80015320 at 96.106800%, with differences attributed to saved-register allocation and instruction scheduling.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that merged or superseded pr-2498 lbmemory matching work.
Search for subsequent changes to lbMemory_80015320 to see how the saved-register allocation and instruction scheduling mismatch was resolved.
Search for subsequent changes to lbMemory_80014FC8 after its 99.324326% partial match.
Inspect whether lbMemory_804318B0 remained a named global or was later moved to a shared header.
Look for later refinements of Handle::x8_hi naming or a split between heap-handle and child-allocation semantics.
Search for other PRs using explicit char arrays for assertion string matching in .data/.sdata.
